purpose: Per-tracker target-project configuration. Records which directory Synapse is currently pointing at as the {project_root} for swarm dispatches. Resolved as the second priority (after explicit --project flag, before agent CWD) by every command. In this repo it points back at the Synapse repo itself — Synapse is operating on its own codebase, building the PKI enrichment feature.
gotchas: When `project_root` equals `{tracker_root}` (as it does here), Synapse is self-targeting — workers write code AND progress files into the same repo, which means runtime artifacts (dashboards/, progress/, etc.) appear as modifications in the same git status as source code changes.
The `set_at` timestamp is ISO-8601 UTC (`2026-04-18T05:29:33Z`) — generated by `!project set` via `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"`. Manually editing this file should preserve the format.
The file is small (5 lines) but is read by EVERY command resolution — treat it as hot-path config.
There is no schema versioning — adding new fields requires updating ProjectService and all readers in lockstep.
`note` is a free-form string used for human bookkeeping (here: 'Synapse target project is itself — building PKI enrichment feature') — not consumed by code; safe to edit.
This file is NOT git-ignored — it is checked in, so the project_root path is repo-specific and will be wrong on any other developer's machine.
patterns: Single-source-of-truth pattern: one file at a known location, read by every command.
Resolution-fallback pattern: explicit flag → this file → CWD (per CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md path-convention sections).
Self-targeting dogfooding: Synapse uses Synapse to build Synapse — proves the architecture works without special-casing.
conventions: Trailing newline omitted in this file.
ISO-8601 UTC timestamps with Z suffix and no fractional seconds.
